RuleDialogTunnelHint: Leave blank to disconnect all tunnels on this network
RuleDialogNoWifi: Not currently connected to a WiFi network.
RuleDialogSsidRequired: Please enter a WiFi network name (SSID).
AlreadyRunningMessage:
MasselGUARD is already running.

To close it: right-click the shield icon in the system tray and choose Exit, or close the main window.

After closing, try starting MasselGUARD again.
SettingsThemeAutoDesc: Choose Light, Dark, or Auto to follow the Windows system preference.
ImportWireGuardPrompt: Select a tunnel from the WireGuard installation:
TunnelMetadataInfo: This tunnel is managed by the WireGuard app. Only group assignment and notes can be edited here — connection settings are stored in the WireGuard profile.
TunnelDialogNotesHint: Optional. Shown as a tooltip on the tunnel name.
TunnelScriptsInfo: Run a .bat or .ps1 file (or embed a script inline) before or after a tunnel connects or disconnects. Scripts run as the current user. Exit code 0 = success.
SettingsRulesInfo: Map a WiFi network name (SSID) to a tunnel. When you connect to that network MasselGUARD automatically activates the tunnel. Leave the tunnel empty to disconnect all tunnels on that network.
SettingsDefaultActionInfo: Choose what happens when you connect to a network that has no matching rule.
SettingsOpenWifiInfo: Automatically activates a tunnel whenever you connect to an open (passwordless) WiFi network — before any rule is evaluated. This protects you on public hotspots.
SettingsGroupsDesc: Organise tunnels into collapsible sections. Drag ↑↓ to reorder. Tunnels not assigned to a group appear under Ungrouped.
SettingsDisableWifiRulesDesc: When on, WiFi rules and the default action are not applied. Connect and disconnect tunnels manually.
SettingsWireGuardDesc: Open WireGuard GUI or view the WireGuard service log
TooltipQuickConnect: Open a .conf file and connect instantly — no import needed
ModeStandaloneDesc: Manages tunnels directly using tunnel.dll + wireguard.dll next to the exe. No WireGuard app required. Tunnels are stored and encrypted locally.
ModeCompanionDesc: Works alongside the official WireGuard for Windows app. Automates connecting and disconnecting your existing WireGuard tunnels based on WiFi rules.
ModeMixedDesc: Both modes active. Manage your own local tunnels AND automate the WireGuard app tunnels side by side.
SettingsSuppressUpdatePrompt: Don't ask to update the installed version at startup
WizardWelcomeBody:
MasselGUARD automatically activates WireGuard tunnels based on your WiFi network.

This wizard takes about a minute. You can re-run it anytime from Settings.
WizardLangBody: Choose your language and preferred colour scheme.
WizardModeBody: How should MasselGUARD manage your WireGuard tunnels?
WizardDoneBody: MasselGUARD is configured and ready. Add tunnels and WiFi rules from the main window.
WizardRulesBody: MasselGUARD uses rules to decide which tunnel to activate when your network changes. You configure rules in Settings after this wizard.
WizardRulesWifiHint: — map a WiFi network name (SSID) to a tunnel
WizardRulesDefaultHint: — what to do when no rule matches
WizardManualBody: MasselGUARD can switch tunnels automatically when your WiFi network changes.
SettingsTrayPopup: Show popup notification when switching tunnels in the background
OrphansDescription: Running WireGuardTunnel$ services that MasselGUARD did not start in this session — usually left over from an improper shutdown. Scan to detect and stop them.
SettingsImportSuccess: Settings imported successfully. Restart may be needed for some changes.
SettingsImportExportInfo: Export your settings to a file to back them up or transfer to another machine. Tunnel configs are not exported — only rules, groups, mode, and automation settings.
SettingsExportWarning:
Settings will be exported without tunnel configurations.

Importing in a newer version of MasselGUARD is not guaranteed to work — some settings may be ignored or reset.
WizardRulesLanHint: — map an Ethernet adapter or DNS suffix to a tunnel
InstallStatusNotInstalled: Not installed — running as standalone portable
InstallAutostart: Do you want MasselGUARD to start automatically when you log in?
InstallRunningFromInstall: Already running from installed location. Reinstall?
UninstallConfirm: Are you sure you want to uninstall MasselGUARD?
InstallPortablePrompt:
A standalone copy of MasselGUARD is running.

Would you like to install it to Program Files for easy access?
UpdateAvailableMsg:
MasselGUARD {0} is available.
You are running {1}.

Would you like to install the update now?
WizardInstallQuestion: Would you like to install MasselGUARD to Program Files, or run it as a portable app?
WizardUpgradeBody: You are running MasselGUARD {0}. The previous version was {1}. This wizard will guide you through any changes.
SettingsHideTunnelCountDesc: Never show the number badge after TUNNELS, even when groups are hidden.
SettingsHideEmptyGroupsDesc: Don't show group tabs that contain no tunnels.
SettingsHideWifiRules: Hide WiFi rules on main window
SettingsHideWifiRulesDesc: When enabled, the WiFi Rules section is not shown on the main window.
WizardShowRulesLabel: Show WiFi rules on main window
WizardShowRulesDesc: Display your rules below the tunnel list for quick reference.
SettingsShowRulesColumnDesc: Shows the number of WiFi rules that reference each tunnel.
WizardImportHint: Restore language, mode, WiFi rules and groups from a .masselguard file
WizardImportSuccess:
Settings imported successfully.
Review the summary below or click Finish.
WizardModeStandaloneDesc: MasselGUARD manages tunnels directly. No WireGuard app needed.
WizardModeCompanionDesc: Automates the WireGuard for Windows app. Requires WireGuard installed.
WizardModeMixedDesc: Both local tunnels and WireGuard profiles side by side.
WizardDisableRulesLabel: Disable WiFi rules — connect tunnels manually
WizardDoneTip: Tip: Right-click any tunnel to mark it as the default action or open network protection.
SettingsStartWithWindowsDesc: Launch MasselGUARD automatically when you log in (via a Scheduled Task).
